title: Does Your Mobile Repair Shop Need Insurance?
author: Mobibix
excerpt: A single shop fire or a burglar clearing out your inventory overnight can bankrupt a business. Learn what insurance policies are strictly necessary for mobile retailers.
content:
## Introduction

You've invested ₹15 Lakh into inventory, built out a beautiful storefront, and hired two talented technicians. You are generating solid monthly profits. 

Then, a short-circuit in an overnight charging station causes a fire. Your entire inventory turns to ash.

Or worse: a technician accidentally punctures a swollen lithium-ion battery during an iPad teardown. The battery ignites, causing severe burns to the technician and destroying the customer's ₹1,00,000 device.

In India, the majority of small mobile shops operate entirely uninsured because owners view it as an "unnecessary expense." This is a gamble you cannot afford. Here is a breakdown of exactly what insurance you need.

---

## 1. Standard Fire and Burglary Insurance (Mandatory)

A mobile shop is a high-value target for burglars. Unlike a grocery store where the inventory is bulky and cheap, a thief can walk out with ₹10 Lakhs worth of smartphones in a single backpack.

**What it covers:** 
Physical loss of your hardware inventory, interior fit-outs, and tools due to theft, fire, or natural disasters (floods).

**The Crucial Detail:** 
To claim a burglary loss, your insurance company will demand proof of what was stolen. A handwritten ledger will get rejected instantly. You must utilize a cloud-based POS system that logs your exact IMEI stock on the date of the theft. By presenting a digitized "Value of Stock" report, the claims process becomes incredibly smooth.

---

## 2. Public Liability Insurance

A customer walks into your newly mopped shop, slips, hits their head on your glass display case, and requires a hospital visit. 

**What it covers:** 
Legal and medical costs if a customer is injured while physically inside your premises.

While personal injury lawsuits are less common in India than the West, they are rising. A simple ₹3,000/year policy protects you against aggressive litigation that could otherwise cripple your business.

---

## 3. Care, Custody, and Control (Bailee's Insurance)

This is highly specific to repair businesses. 

When a customer hands you their ₹1,30,000 MacBook Pro for repair, you are legally taking "custody" of it. Standard commercial property insurance covers *your* goods, but it actively excludes goods belonging to others.

If your shop burns down overnight, standard fire insurance pays for your tools, but the customer will sue you for the value of their destroyed MacBook.

**What it covers:** 
The exact value of customer devices currently checked into your care for repair.

*(Note: Always enforce a digital waiver on your intake Job Cards stating you are not liable for lost data, only hardware value.)*

---

## 4. Cyber and Data Breach Insurance (Emerging Risk)

Your technicians routinely handle devices containing sensitive corporate data, unencrypted photos, and crypto wallets.

If an employee acts maliciously and steals data off a customer's phone during a "diagnostic," the customer will unleash an FIR and sue the business owner. Cyber insurance protects you legally from data-related liability claims.

---

## Conclusion

Insurance is not an expense; it is a shield that guarantees your business survives a catastrophe.

You cannot protect what you cannot quantify. A foundational requirement for getting any loss paid out quickly is a pristine digital paper trail of your inventory and customer belongings.
